summary: Created a planning-only, dependency-ordered M6 Alpha system-complete task DAG. The graph covers start-to-victory playability, diplomacy, legitimacy, succession completion, layered AI, policy/event/encyclopedia surfaces, three-scenario data pipeline, map candidate path, batch auto-run and balance dashboard, accessibility core flows, adviser intervention evidence, Alpha no P0/P1 validation, and M6 exit validation without implementing product code.

tests_run.result: Modified allowed coordination/status files are present; new untracked M6 task files and this systems handoff are present under allowed paths. Pre-existing untracked ui-samples/ remains and was not touched.

tests_run.command: node -e authored changed-path scope guard for M6-TASK-GRAPH-001
tests_run.result: scope guard ok authoredPaths=21 ignoredPreexisting=3; authored paths are limited to allowed task/status/message paths and do not include forbidden apps/packages/content-source/tools/CI/docs/gate/roadmap paths.
M6-TASK-GRAPH-001 is REVIEW; M6-ALPHA-SYSTEM-CONTRACT-001 is READY but dependency-gated by the REVIEW graph; the other 13 downstream M6 tasks are DRAFT.
No dependency-ready output while M6-TASK-GRAPH-001 remains in REVIEW.

tests_run.command: node -e taskctl validate for every M6 task file
tests_run.result: taskctl validate all M6 ok count=15.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON validation for all new M6 task files and DAG acyclicity/dependency/status shape
tests_run.result: M6 JSON DAG check ok files=15 edges=41 graphStatus=REVIEW firstReady=M6-ALPHA-SYSTEM-CONTRACT-001 downstreamDraft=13.

decisions: Created a minimal M6 DAG with 14 downstream tasks: Alpha contract, scenario data pipeline, map candidate pipeline, diplomacy/legitimacy, succession completion, policy/event runtime, start-to-victory sim loop, layered AI, client Alpha surfaces, accessibility core flows, batch auto-run/balance dashboard, adviser intervention evidence, Alpha no P0/P1 validation, and M6 exit validation.

Set M6-ALPHA-SYSTEM-CONTRACT-001 as the first executable downstream task with status READY; it remains dependency-gated until M6-TASK-GRAPH-001 is accepted/closed. All other downstream M6 tasks remain DRAFT.

Preserved DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE exactly: no manual node battle implementation task exists, no blocking manual node battle spike/research task exists for M6/M7/M8, and manual node battle is only mentioned as a post-1.0 non-blocking candidate note.

Kept the graph planning-only. No apps, packages, content-source, tools, CI, dependency manifests, roadmap, acceptance matrix, prior gate docs, accepted ADRs, release artifacts, telemetry, server/multiplayer behavior, arbitrary-code mod support, or frozen product/platform/core architecture decisions were modified.

Split downstream scopes to keep sim-core decoupled from React/Pixi/Electron/Node IO, preserve single-writer authority, same GameCommand paths for player and AI, explicit schemas, deterministic ordering, and stable IDs.

No ADR is required for this graph because it does not change architecture; downstream tasks must write ADRs before architecture, save-format, protocol, platform, dependency, or irreversible migration changes.
risks: M6 is broad; several downstream tasks are R4 because they will later touch diplomacy, legitimacy, succession, start-to-victory simulation, layered AI, save/protocol compatibility, and milestone validation.
Three-scenario and map candidate work has historical/cultural risk and must keep source labels, uncertainty labels, and review routing before formal content acceptance.
Client Alpha surfaces and balance dashboard can easily duplicate simulation rules or become hidden telemetry; their scopes explicitly require read-model-only UI and local-only evidence.
Pre-existing untracked ui-samples/ remains in the workspace and was not touched by this task.
requested_action: Perform independent QA review of the M6 task graph for roadmap/acceptance-matrix coverage, dependency ordering, first-ready/downstream-DRAFT status, DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE preservation, allowed/forbidden path discipline, architecture-boundary safety, and handoff validity. Accept or request changes.
